ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "surname" TEXT /* Surname of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the customer */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the customer */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the customer */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the customer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "credit_score" INTEGER /* Credit score of the customer */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the customer */,
  "tenure" INTEGER /* Number of years the customer has been with the bank */,
  "balance" REAL /* Current balance in the customer's account */,
  "num_of_products" INTEGER /* Number of products the customer has with the bank */,
  "has_cr_card" INTEGER /* Whether the customer has a credit card */,
  "is_active_member" INTEGER /* Whether the customer is an active member */,
  "estimated_salary" REAL /* Estimated salary of the customer */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date the customer joined the bank */,
  "last_activity_date" TEXT /* Date of the last activity (e.g., transaction, interaction) */,
  "churn_status" INTEGER /* Current churn status of the customer (e.g., active, churned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_geography" (
  "geography_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each geography category */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "geography_category" TEXT /* Category of the customer's geography (e.g., urban, rural, suburban) */,
  "geography_detail" TEXT /* More detailed information about the customer's geography */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the customer's location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the customer's location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("geography_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_geography_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Category of the customer's age group (e.g., 18-24, 25-34, 35-44) */,
  "age_range_start" INTEGER /* Start age of the age group */,
  "age_range_end" INTEGER /* End age of the age group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_age_groups_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_credit_score_groups" (
  "credit_score_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each credit score group */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "credit_score_group" TEXT /* Category of the customer's credit score group (e.g., excellent, good, fair) */,
  "credit_score_range_start" INTEGER /* Start credit score of the credit score group */,
  "credit_score_range_end" INTEGER /* End credit score of the credit score group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("credit_score_group_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_credit_score_groups_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic category */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the customer */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level of the customer */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Employment status of the customer */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the customer */,
  "income_range" TEXT /* Income range of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_demographics_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_accounts" (
  "account_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each account */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "account_type" TEXT /* Type of account (e.g., checking, savings, credit card) */,
  "account_balance" REAL /* Current balance in the account */,
  "account_open_date" TEXT /* Date the account was opened */,
  "account_close_date" TEXT /* Date the account was closed, if applicable */,
  "account_status" TEXT /* Status of the account (e.g., active, closed, dormant) */,
  "account_type_description" TEXT /* More detailed description of the account type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("account_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_accounts_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_transactions" (
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transaction */,
  "account_id" INTEGER /* ID of the account */,
  "transaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the transaction */,
  "transaction_amount" REAL /* Amount of the transaction */,
  "transaction_type" TEXT /* Type of transaction (e.g., deposit, withdrawal, transfer) */,
  "transaction_description" TEXT /* Description of the transaction */,
  "transaction_channel" TEXT /* Channel through which the transaction was made (e.g., online, ATM, branch) */,
  "transaction_status" TEXT /* Status of the transaction (e.g., completed, pending, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_transactions_account_id FOREIGN KEY ("account_id") REFERENCES customer_accounts ("account_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_churn" (
  "churn_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each churn prediction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "churn_probability" REAL /* Predicted probability of the customer churning */,
  "churn_reason" TEXT /* Reason for churn, if applicable */,
  "churn_date" TEXT /* Date the customer churned */,
  "churn_prediction_date" TEXT /* Date when the churn prediction was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("churn_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_churn_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the interaction */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., phone call, email, visit) */,
  "interaction_channel" TEXT /* Channel through which the interaction took place (e.g., phone, email, branch) */,
  "interaction_outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the interaction (e.g., issue resolved, complaint, positive feedback) */,
  "interaction_notes" TEXT /* Detailed notes about the interaction for future reference */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_interactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, marketing, sales) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user for security */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  "last_password_change_date" TEXT /* Date the user last changed their password */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
